description: MiniPanel — A self-hosted analytics platform for product teams. Covers Foundation (event collection, identity resolution, sample data, app shell), MVP (event exploration, trend analysis), MMP (numeric aggregations, dimensional breakdowns, comparative charts, funnel analysis, user profiles, visual polish), and Nice-to-haves (saved analyses, autocomplete, multi-event comparison).

userStories.title: Write test scaffolding: Playwright config and shared helpers
userStories.description: Create the Playwright configuration and shared API helper functions that all test files will use. Write ONLY test infrastructure files — no application code.
acceptanceCriteria: playwright.config.ts exists at the project root with: baseURL 'http://localhost:5173', actionTimeout 5000ms, navigationTimeout 10000ms, webServer configured to run 'npm run dev' and wait for http://localhost:5173, reuseExistingServer: true
e2e/helpers.ts exists and exports these functions using Playwright's APIRequestContext against http://localhost:3001 (direct to Express, not through Vite proxy):
createEvent(request, { event, device_id?, user_id?, timestamp?, properties? }) → POST /api/events, returns APIResponse
createBatchEvents(request, events[]) → POST /api/events/batch, returns APIResponse
getEvents(request, filters?: { event_name?, user_id?, device_id?, start_date?, end_date?, limit?, offset? }) → GET /api/events with query params, returns APIResponse
getEventNames(request) → GET /api/events/names, returns APIResponse
getStatsOverview(request) → GET /api/stats/overview, returns APIResponse
getUserProfile(request, id) → GET /api/users/:id, returns APIResponse
All helpers return the raw Playwright APIResponse so tests can assert on both status codes and response body
TypeScript compiles with no errors: npx tsc --noEmit passes on all e2e/ files
git commit with message 'test: add Playwright config and shared API helpers'
userStories.notes: This is test infrastructure only. The helpers define the API contract that the implementation must fulfill. Use the Playwright test() and expect() imports from @playwright/test.

userStories.title: Write API contract tests for all endpoints
userStories.description: Write Playwright tests for all API endpoints using the request fixture (no browser). Tests self-seed their own data via the helpers in beforeAll/beforeEach hooks. Write ONLY test files — no application code.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/api-events.spec.ts contains tests for POST /api/events and POST /api/events/batch:
- POST /api/events with all fields (event, device_id, user_id, timestamp, properties) returns 201 with stored event including generated id
- POST /api/events with minimal fields (event + device_id only) returns 201
- POST /api/events with minimal fields (event + user_id only) returns 201
- POST /api/events missing event name returns 400 with { error } body
- POST /api/events missing both device_id and user_id returns 400 with { error } body
- POST /api/events with omitted timestamp returns 201 and response contains a server-set ISO 8601 timestamp
- POST /api/events with properties object returns 201 and properties round-trip as object (not stringified JSON)
- POST /api/events/batch with array of valid events returns 200 with { accepted: N, errors: [] }
- POST /api/events/batch with mix of valid and invalid events returns 200, accepted count = valid count, errors array has { index, message } for each invalid event
- POST /api/events/batch with empty array returns 400
- POST /api/events/batch with >1000 events returns 400
e2e/api-queries.spec.ts contains tests for GET /api/events, GET /api/events/names, GET /api/stats/overview:
- GET /api/events with no params returns { events, total, limit: 50, offset: 0 }
- GET /api/events returns events sorted by timestamp descending (newest first)
- GET /api/events?event_name=X returns only events matching that name
- GET /api/events?start_date=X&end_date=Y returns only events within the date range (inclusive)
- GET /api/events?limit=10&offset=5 returns correct page slice
- GET /api/events with combined filters (event_name + date range) applies AND logic
- GET /api/events/names returns alphabetically sorted array of distinct event name strings
- GET /api/stats/overview returns { total_events, total_users, event_counts_by_name, date_range: { earliest, latest } }
- GET /api/stats/overview total_users counts resolved identities (device mapped to user is not double-counted)
e2e/api-users.spec.ts contains tests for GET /api/users/:id:
- GET /api/users/:id with known user_id returns { user_id, device_ids: string[], total_events, first_seen, last_seen }
- GET /api/users/:id with device_id that has a mapping returns the resolved user profile
- GET /api/users/:id with unknown ID returns 404 with { error } body
All test files compile: npx tsc --noEmit passes
npx playwright test --list lists all API test cases without errors
git commit with message 'test: add API contract tests for events, queries, and users'
userStories.notes: Tests seed their own data using createEvent() and createBatchEvents() helpers in beforeAll hooks. Each test file should create fresh data with unique identifiers to avoid cross-test interference. API tests use the Playwright request fixture directly — no browser needed.

userStories.title: Write identity resolution E2E tests
userStories.description: Write the BR-101 identity resolution verification scenarios as API-level Playwright tests. These are the most critical tests — they verify the core invariant of the system. Tests self-seed via API helpers. Write ONLY test files — no application code.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/identity.spec.ts contains exactly 4 test cases:

Test 1 — Retroactive merge: create 4 events with device_id='test-device-X' and no user_id, then create 1 event with device_id='test-device-X' AND user_id='test-user-Y' (this triggers the identity mapping), then GET /api/events?user_id=test-user-Y and assert all 5 events are returned

Test 2 — Multi-device merge: create events for device_id='test-device-A' (no user_id), create events for device_id='test-device-B' (no user_id), create event linking test-device-A to test-user-Z, create event linking test-device-B to test-user-Z, then GET /api/events?user_id=test-user-Z and assert events from both devices appear

Test 3 — Collision rejection: create event with device_id='test-device-C' and user_id='test-user-P' (creates mapping), then create event with device_id='test-device-C' and user_id='test-user-Q', assert second request returns 409 status

Test 4 — Unresolved device: create events for device_id='test-device-D' with no user_id (never create a mapping), then GET /api/events?device_id=test-device-D, assert only test-device-D events are returned
Test file compiles: npx tsc --noEmit passes
npx playwright test --list lists all 4 identity test cases
git commit with message 'test: add identity resolution E2E tests (BR-101 scenarios)'
userStories.notes: Use unique test- prefixed IDs in each test to avoid collisions with other test files. Each test should be independent — seed its own data in beforeEach or at the start of the test. These tests mirror the 'How to verify' section from the business requirements.

userStories.title: Write UI E2E tests for Events page
userStories.description: Write browser-level Playwright tests for the event explorer page. Tests self-seed data via API helpers in beforeAll (create events with varied names, timestamps, properties, and identities — enough for filter, pagination, and expand assertions). Write ONLY test files — no application code.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/ui-events.spec.ts contains a beforeAll hook that seeds test data via the API: at least 60 events across 3+ event types with varied timestamps and properties, using createEvent() or createBatchEvents() helpers
Test: Events page loads at '/' and renders a table containing event data
Test: Table displays columns — Timestamp, Event Name, User identity, Properties preview
Test: Filter by event name using a dropdown/select updates the table to show only matching events
Test: Filter by date range (start and end date inputs) updates the table
Test: Pagination — Next button loads the next page, Previous button returns to prior page
Test: Clicking a table row expands to show all event properties as key-value pairs
Test: Empty state message appears when filters match no events
Test: Loading indicator is visible while data is being fetched
Test file compiles: npx tsc --noEmit passes
npx playwright test --list lists all Events page test cases
git commit with message 'test: add UI E2E tests for Events explorer page'
userStories.notes: The beforeAll data seeding uses the API helpers which POST to http://localhost:3001 directly. Use data-testid attributes in selectors where possible for resilience. For the empty state test, filter by a non-existent event name. The pagination test needs at least 51 events (default page size is 50) to verify Next works.

userStories.title: Write UI E2E tests for navigation and user lookup
userStories.description: Write browser-level Playwright tests for the app shell navigation and user lookup page. User lookup tests self-seed via API in beforeAll: create a user with device mapping, events from both anonymous and identified phases. Write ONLY test files — no application code.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/ui-navigation.spec.ts contains tests:
- Sidebar displays 'MiniPanel' title text
- Sidebar has navigation links for Events, Users, Funnels, Settings
- Clicking each nav link navigates to the correct route ('/', '/users', '/funnels', '/settings')
- Active navigation item has visually distinct styling (check for an active class or different aria attribute)
- Funnels and Settings routes display placeholder content
e2e/ui-users.spec.ts contains a beforeAll hook that seeds: multiple users with events across different devices, including at least one device-to-user identity mapping, via the API helpers
e2e/ui-users.spec.ts contains tests:
- Users page at '/users' shows a search input and a table of users
- Users table displays columns: User ID, Devices (count), Events (count), Last seen
- Users table rows are clickable — clicking a row navigates to '/users/:id' showing the user profile
- User profile shows a 'Back to Users' link that returns to the users table
- User profile displays the resolved identity heading ('User: <id>') and a list of associated device IDs
- User profile shows an event timeline in chronological order (oldest first) with timestamp, event name, and expanded properties
- Typing 2+ characters in the search input shows an autocomplete dropdown with matching user_ids and device_ids
- Selecting an autocomplete result navigates to that user's profile
- Pressing Enter with search text filters the users table to matching rows
- Empty state message appears when search matches no users
All test files compile: npx tsc --noEmit passes
npx playwright test --list lists all navigation and user lookup test cases
git commit with message 'test: add UI E2E tests for navigation and user lookup'
userStories.notes: Navigation tests don't need seeded data — they test the shell layout and routing. User lookup tests need multiple users and an identity resolution scenario seeded via API. The users table is the default view; clicking a row navigates to the profile detail. Use data-testid on search input, table rows, and back link for resilient selectors.

userStories.title: Write API contract tests for trends and property metadata
userStories.description: Write Playwright API tests for the trend aggregation endpoint and property metadata endpoint. Tests self-seed data via API helpers. Write ONLY test files — no application code.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/api-trends.spec.ts contains tests for GET /api/trends:
- GET /api/trends with valid event_name returns { event_name, granularity, start_date, end_date, data: [{ date, total_count, unique_users }] }
- GET /api/trends without event_name returns 400
- GET /api/trends with granularity='day' returns daily buckets
- GET /api/trends with granularity='week' returns weekly buckets
- GET /api/trends returns zero-filled buckets for days with no events (no gaps in series)
- GET /api/trends unique_users uses resolved identities (device mapped to user not double-counted)
- GET /api/trends with measure=sum&property=amount returns { data: [{ date, value }] } with summed values
- GET /api/trends with measure=avg&property=amount returns averaged values
- GET /api/trends with measure=sum but no property param returns 400
- GET /api/trends with measure=sum on non-numeric property returns 400
- GET /api/trends with breakdown_by=property returns { series: [{ key, data }] } with top 5 + __other__
- GET /api/trends without breakdown_by returns flat data array (backward compatible)
e2e/api-properties.spec.ts contains tests for GET /api/events/:eventName/properties:
- Returns array of { name, type: 'string'|'number'|'boolean', sample_values }
- Correctly detects numeric properties (amount, quantity) vs string properties (url, button_name)
- Returns 404 or empty array for unknown event name
All test files compile: npx tsc --noEmit passes
npx playwright test --list lists all trend and property API test cases
git commit with message 'test: add API contract tests for trends and property metadata'
userStories.notes: Seed events with known numeric properties (amount, quantity) and string properties (url, button_name) so type detection can be verified. Use unique test- prefixed identifiers.

userStories.title: Write API contract tests for funnels and saved analyses
userStories.description: Write Playwright API tests for the funnel analysis endpoint and saved analyses CRUD. Tests self-seed data via API helpers. Write ONLY test files — no application code.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/api-funnels.spec.ts contains tests for POST /api/funnels/query:
- Valid funnel with 3 steps returns { steps: [{ event_name, count, conversion_rate, drop_off }], overall_conversion_rate }
- steps[0].conversion_rate is always 1.0
- Conversion counts decrease or stay equal across steps (count[N] <= count[N-1])
- Step order enforced: user who does step 2 before step 1 (by timestamp) is NOT counted as completing step 2
- Identity resolution: user does step 1 as anonymous device, identifies, does step 2 — counts as one user progressing
- Fewer than 2 steps returns 400
- More than 5 steps returns 400
e2e/api-saved.spec.ts contains tests for saved analyses CRUD:
- POST /api/saved-analyses with { name, type: 'trend', config } returns 201 with saved analysis including id
- GET /api/saved-analyses returns array sorted by updated_at descending
- GET /api/saved-analyses/:id returns the specific saved analysis
- DELETE /api/saved-analyses/:id returns 200 and subsequent GET returns 404
All test files compile: npx tsc --noEmit passes
npx playwright test --list lists all funnel and saved analyses test cases
git commit with message 'test: add API contract tests for funnels and saved analyses'
userStories.notes: For funnel tests, seed events for multiple users with known step sequences and timestamps. Include an identity resolution scenario where a user starts anonymous and identifies between steps.

userStories.title: Write UI E2E tests for Trends page
userStories.description: Write browser-level Playwright tests for the Trends page including controls, chart rendering, measure/breakdown selectors, and chart type switching. Tests self-seed data via API helpers in beforeAll. Write ONLY test files — no application code.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/ui-trends.spec.ts contains a beforeAll hook that seeds events via API: at least 2 event types with numeric properties, spread over multiple days
Test: Trends page is accessible from sidebar navigation
Test: Default state shows 'Select an event to see its trend' prompt
Test: Selecting an event from dropdown renders a chart (SVG or canvas element visible)
Test: Changing granularity to 'Week' updates the chart (fewer data points)
Test: Changing date range preset updates the chart
Test: Chart has a tooltip visible on hover with date and values
Test: Chart has a legend identifying the series
Test: Selecting measure 'Sum' shows a property dropdown
Test: Selecting measure 'Total Count' hides the property dropdown
Test: Selecting a breakdown property shows multiple series in the chart legend
Test: Selecting 'None' for breakdown returns to single-series view
Test: Clicking bar chart icon switches chart type (different chart elements rendered)
Test: Clicking area chart icon switches chart type
Test: Multi-event selection shows multiple series (one per event)
Test file compiles: npx tsc --noEmit passes
npx playwright test --list lists all Trends page test cases
git commit with message 'test: add UI E2E tests for Trends page'
userStories.notes: Use data-testid attributes for chart type toggles, measure/breakdown selectors. The chart rendering tests should check for the presence of SVG elements, not exact pixel values.

userStories.title: Write UI E2E tests for Funnels page and enhanced user profile
userStories.description: Write browser-level Playwright tests for the funnel analysis page and the enhanced user profile. Tests self-seed data via API helpers in beforeAll. Write ONLY test files — no application code.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/ui-funnels.spec.ts contains a beforeAll hook that seeds events for a multi-step funnel scenario via API
Test: Funnels page accessible from sidebar, shows 'Funnel Analysis' heading
Test: Step builder shows 2 initial step dropdowns with event name options
Test: Add Step button adds a new step (up to 5), disabled at 5 steps
Test: Remove Step button removes a step (minimum 2), disabled at 2 steps
Test: Clicking 'Analyze' with valid steps shows funnel visualization with conversion rates
Test: Funnel visualization shows decreasing bar sizes and conversion percentages
Test: Overall conversion rate is displayed
Test: Empty state when no users match the funnel
e2e/ui-users-enhanced.spec.ts contains a beforeAll hook that seeds a user with multiple devices and anonymous + identified events
Test: Navigating to '/users' shows the users table, clicking a seeded user row navigates to their profile
Test: User profile shows breadcrumb 'Users › {user_id}' and 'Back to Users' link
Test: User profile shows identity cluster with resolved user_id and linked device_ids
Test: Event timeline shows events in chronological order with source identity labels
Test: Anonymous events (pre-merge) have a visual indicator
Test: Timeline supports pagination or 'Load more'
Test: 'Back to Users' link returns to the users table
All test files compile: npx tsc --noEmit passes
npx playwright test --list lists all funnel and user profile test cases
git commit with message 'test: add UI E2E tests for Funnels page and enhanced user profile'
userStories.notes: Funnel tests need users who progressed through multiple steps. Seed at least 10 users with step 1, 7 with step 2, 3 with step 3 for realistic conversion rates. User profile tests need a multi-device identity scenario.

userStories.title: Initialize project with monorepo structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolded with the chosen tech stack so that all subsequent stories have a working build pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Project root contains a package.json with a single 'npm run dev' command that starts both backend and frontend concurrently
Backend: Express.js + TypeScript in a 'server/' directory, compiles and starts on port 3001
Frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript + TailwindCSS in a 'client/' directory, starts on port 5173 and proxies API requests to backend
Shadcn UI initialized in client with at least one component (Button) available
lucide-react installed and importable in client
Zod installed and importable in server
Playwright installed with existing test files in 'e2e/' intact — do NOT modify or delete any files in e2e/
A single 'npm install' at the root installs all dependencies (workspaces or similar)
README.md documents: 'npm install && npm run dev' as the single start command
Typecheck passes for both client and server
Playwright config is valid: npx playwright test --list succeeds
userStories.notes: This is the skeleton. Keep it minimal — no business logic yet. Use npm workspaces or a simple concurrently setup.

userStories.title: Set up SQLite database with connection and schema management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SQLite database that initializes automatically on startup so that subsequent stories can persist data.
acceptanceCriteria: Server uses better-sqlite3 (synchronous) or sqlite3 package to create/open a local 'minipanel.db' file in the project root
Database file is gitignored
A schema initialization function runs on server startup and creates tables if they do not exist

Events table created with columns: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT), event_name (TEXT NOT NULL), device_id (TEXT), user_id (TEXT), timestamp (TEXT NOT NULL), properties (TEXT, stores JSON string)
Identity mappings table created with columns: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT), device_id (TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE), user_id (TEXT NOT NULL), created_at (TEXT NOT NULL)
Server starts successfully with the database initialized and logs a confirmation message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use better-sqlite3 for simplicity (synchronous API, no native build issues on most systems). Store properties as a JSON string. The UNIQUE constraint on device_id in the identity_mappings table enforces the one-device-to-one-user rule.

userStories.title: Implement event ingestion API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer instrumenting an app, I need to POST events to MiniPanel so they are stored and available for querying.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/events accepts a JSON body with fields: event (string, required), device_id (string, optional), user_id (string, optional), timestamp (string, optional ISO 8601), properties (object, optional)
Request body is validated with Zod: event name is required and non-empty, at least one of device_id or user_id must be present
If timestamp is omitted, server sets it to current UTC time in ISO 8601 format
Valid events are inserted into the events table and return 201 with the stored event object including its generated id
Invalid events return 400 with a JSON error body containing a human-readable message describing what is missing or wrong
If the event contains both device_id and user_id, an identity mapping is created or confirmed (INSERT OR IGNORE into identity_mappings)

If the event contains both device_id and user_id, and that device_id is already mapped to a DIFFERENT user_id in identity_mappings, reject the event with HTTP 409 and body: { error: 'device_id is already mapped to a different user' }. Do NOT insert the event.
Properties object is serialized to a JSON string before storage
Typecheck passes
Playwright single-event POST tests pass: npx playwright test api-events.spec.ts (the POST /api/events tests)
userStories.notes: Identity mapping creation here is a side effect of event ingestion — it's the core of the simplified merge model. INSERT OR IGNORE handles the case where the mapping already exists. Check for identity conflict BEFORE inserting the event.

userStories.title: Implement batch event ingestion endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to send multiple events in a single request so that high-volume ingestion is efficient.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/events/batch accepts a JSON body with field: events (array of event objects, same schema as single event)
Validated with Zod: array must have at least 1 event and at most 1000
Each event in the array is validated individually with the same rules as POST /api/events
All valid events in the batch are inserted in a single SQLite transaction for performance
Identity mappings are created for any events with both device_id and user_id

If an event in the batch would create a device_id mapping that conflicts with an existing mapping (device already mapped to a different user), that individual event is skipped and reported in the errors array — it does NOT abort the entire batch
Returns 200 with a JSON body: { accepted: number, errors: Array<{ index: number, message: string }> }
If any individual event fails validation or has an identity conflict, it is skipped (not inserted) and reported in the errors array, but the rest of the batch succeeds
Typecheck passes
All Playwright event API tests pass: npx playwright test api-events.spec.ts (single + batch tests)
userStories.notes: This is essential for the sample data seeder (US-008) to run fast. Wrap the whole insert loop in a transaction.

userStories.title: Implement identity resolution query logic
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need all queries to use resolved identities so that anonymous and known events for the same person are unified.
acceptanceCriteria: A resolveIdentity(deviceOrUserId: string) function exists that: given a user_id, returns that user_id; given a device_id, looks up the identity_mappings table and returns the mapped user_id if one exists, otherwise returns the device_id as-is

A getEventsForUser(userId: string) function exists that returns all events attributed to a resolved user: events where user_id matches, PLUS events where device_id is mapped to that user_id via identity_mappings

GET /api/events?user_id=<id> returns all resolved events for that user, sorted by timestamp ascending (oldest first — this is the user timeline view, distinct from the paginated list in US-007 which sorts descending/newest first)
GET /api/events?device_id=<id> resolves the device to a user (if mapping exists) and returns all events for that resolved user, sorted by timestamp ascending
Response includes an array of event objects with parsed properties (JSON object, not string)
The merge is retroactive: events recorded before the identity mapping was created are included
Typecheck passes
All identity resolution tests pass: npx playwright test identity.spec.ts
userStories.notes: This is the most critical story. The SQL query for getEventsForUser needs to join events with identity_mappings: SELECT * FROM events WHERE user_id = ? OR device_id IN (SELECT device_id FROM identity_mappings WHERE user_id = ?). Test the retroactive merge carefully.

userStories.title: Write automated tests for identity resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated tests that verify identity resolution correctness so that the most critical invariant of the system is protected against regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file exists at server/src/__tests__/identity-resolution.test.ts (or similar path using vitest or jest)
Test runner is configured and 'npm test' in the server workspace runs the tests
Test 1 - Retroactive merge: send 4 anonymous events for device-X, then send 1 event with both device-X and user-Y, query events for user-Y, assert all 5 events are returned
Test 2 - Multi-device merge: send anonymous events for device-A and device-B, link device-A to user-Z, link device-B to user-Z, query events for user-Z, assert events from both devices appear
Test 3 - Device collision rejection: link device-C to user-P, attempt to link device-C to user-Q, assert the second mapping is rejected (409 or error)
Test 4 - Unidentified device: send anonymous events for device-D (no mapping), query by device-D, assert only device-D events are returned with device-D as the identity
All tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These tests directly mirror the 'How to verify' section of BR-101. Use a fresh in-memory SQLite database per test to avoid test pollution. This satisfies the hard constraint: 'The codebase must include at least one automated test that verifies identity resolution.'

userStories.title: Implement event listing API with filters
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need to query events by name, time range, and identity so I can explore what happened in my product.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/events returns paginated events sorted by timestamp descending (newest first)

Supports query parameters: event_name (filter by exact event name), user_id (resolved identity filter), device_id (resolved identity filter), start_date (ISO 8601, inclusive), end_date (ISO 8601, inclusive), limit (default 50, max 1000), offset (default 0)
All filters are optional and combinable
Response body: { events: Event[], total: number, limit: number, offset: number }
Events in response have properties parsed from JSON string to object
Identity resolution is applied: if filtering by user_id, events from all mapped devices are included
Typecheck passes
GET /api/events filter and pagination tests pass in api-queries.spec.ts (the GET /api/events/names and GET /api/stats/overview tests may still fail — those are addressed in US-011)
userStories.notes: This is the read API that powers the frontend event explorer. Build the SQL query dynamically based on which filters are provided.

userStories.title: Build sample data seeder script
userStories.description: As a new user trying MiniPanel, I need realistic demo data loaded so I can explore the product without instrumenting my own app first.
acceptanceCriteria: A script at 'scripts/seed.ts' (or similar) can be run via 'npm run seed' from the project root
Generates at least 5 distinct event types: Page Viewed, Button Clicked, Sign Up Completed, Purchase Completed, Subscription Renewed
Generates at least 50 resolved users (users with a user_id) and additional anonymous-only device IDs
Generates at least 10,000 events with timestamps in the last 30 days relative to the current date at seed time (NOT hardcoded dates — use Date.now() as the anchor)
Distribution is non-uniform: some users have 500+ events, most have 50-200, power-law or similar distribution
Event types are non-uniform: Page Viewed is most common (~40%), Button Clicked (~25%), Sign Up Completed (~15%), Purchase Completed (~12%), Subscription Renewed (~8%)
Includes identity resolution scenarios: at least 20 users start as anonymous (device-only) and later identify, at least 5 users have 2+ devices, at least 10 device IDs never get mapped to a user
Events include string properties (url, referrer, button_name, plan_type) and numeric properties (amount, duration_seconds, quantity) appropriate to each event type
Seeder calls the batch API endpoint (POST /api/events/batch) or directly inserts into the database
Running the seeder twice does not duplicate data (clears existing data first or is idempotent)
After seeding, the script MUST verify the total count: query SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events and assert >= 10,000 rows were inserted. Print the count to stdout.
Typecheck passes
After seeding, existing query tests still pass: npx playwright test api-queries.spec.ts
userStories.notes: The seeder should be deterministic (use a fixed seed for the random number generator) so results are reproducible. Direct database insertion is fine and will be much faster than HTTP calls for 10k events. All timestamps MUST be relative to now — never hardcode dates. The verification count at the end is mandatory to catch silent insertion failures.

userStories.title: Build application shell with navigation layout
userStories.description: As a user opening MiniPanel, I need a clean application shell with sidebar navigation so I can move between the main areas of the product.
acceptanceCriteria: App renders a responsive layout with a fixed sidebar on the left and a main content area on the right

Sidebar contains navigation links with lucide-react icons for: Events (Activity icon), Funnels (Filter icon, links to placeholder), Users (Users icon, links to placeholder), Settings (Settings icon, links to placeholder)
React Router is set up with routes for each navigation item
Active navigation item is visually highlighted
Placeholder pages display the page title in the main content area for routes not yet implemented
Application title 'MiniPanel' appears at the top of the sidebar
Layout uses Tailwind CSS and looks clean on screens 1024px and wider
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Navigation tests pass: npx playwright test ui-navigation.spec.ts
userStories.notes: Use shadcn/ui components where appropriate (e.g., Button for nav items, or a simple custom sidebar). Keep it minimal — this is the shell, not the feature pages. Funnels, Users, and Settings are placeholders for future tiers.

userStories.title: Build event explorer page with table and filters
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need a page where I can browse events, filter by name and time range, and see event details so I can verify data and explore user behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Events page is accessible from the sidebar navigation
Displays a table of events with columns: Timestamp, Event Name, User (resolved identity), Properties (truncated preview)
Table loads data from GET /api/events on mount
Filter bar above the table with: event name dropdown (populated from distinct event names in the data), date range picker (start and end date inputs), a search/filter button
Filters update the API query parameters and reload the table
Clicking a row expands it or opens a detail panel showing all event properties formatted as key-value pairs
Pagination controls at the bottom (Previous / Next) using offset-based pagination
Loading state shown while fetching data
Empty state shown when no events match the filters
Uses shadcn/ui Table component and lucide-react icons
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Events page UI tests pass: npx playwright test ui-events.spec.ts
userStories.notes: This is the primary interface for the developer persona (verifying event flow) and the analyst persona (exploring data). Keep the UI functional, not flashy.

userStories.title: Add distinct event names and basic stats API endpoints
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need API endpoints that return distinct event names and basic event counts so the UI filter dropdowns and summary stats can be populated.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/events/names returns an array of distinct event names sorted alphabetically

GET /api/stats/overview returns a JSON object with: total_events (number), total_users (number of distinct resolved user identities), event_counts_by_name (object mapping event name to count), date_range (object with earliest and latest event timestamps)
Total users count uses resolved identities: anonymous devices mapped to a user are not counted separately
Responses are fast (< 500ms) on the sample dataset of 10k events
Typecheck passes
Query API tests pass (names + stats): npx playwright test api-queries.spec.ts
userStories.notes: These endpoints support the event explorer filters and could support a future dashboard. Keep the SQL simple — COUNT, DISTINCT, GROUP BY.

userStories.title: Add users list page and user profile detail
userStories.description: As a support lead, I need a browsable list of all known users so I can find and inspect any user's complete event history without memorizing IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Users page at '/users' is accessible from the sidebar navigation
Default view is a table of all known users with columns: User ID, Devices (count of linked devices), Events (total count), Last seen
Unresolved devices (no user mapping) appear in the table with their device_id directly
Table supports pagination (page size 50)
Search input above the table has autocomplete: typing 2+ characters queries GET /api/users/search?q=<term> and shows a dropdown of matching user_ids and device_ids (each labeled 'user' or 'device')
GET /api/users/search?q=<term> returns { results: [{ id, type: 'user'|'device' }] } with prefix-matched user_ids and device_ids (limit 10)
Selecting an autocomplete result navigates directly to that user's profile at '/users/:id'; pressing Enter filters the table
GET /api/users endpoint returns paginated list: { users: [{ user_id, device_ids, total_events, last_seen }], total, limit, offset }
GET /api/users accepts optional query param ?q=<term> to filter the list by partial user_id or device_id match
Clicking a user row navigates to '/users/:id' showing the user profile detail
User profile shows resolved identity at the top: 'User: <resolved_id>' with a list of associated device IDs
User profile shows a chronological event timeline (oldest first) with: timestamp, event name, all properties expanded
A 'Back to Users' link at the top returns to the users table
GET /api/users/:id endpoint returns user profile data: resolved user_id, list of mapped device_ids, total event count, first_seen and last_seen timestamps
Empty state in table when search matches no users; 404 page when navigating to unknown user ID
Uses shadcn/ui components (Input, Card, Table)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
User lookup tests pass: npx playwright test ui-users.spec.ts and npx playwright test api-users.spec.ts
userStories.notes: This serves the support lead persona. Two-level view: users table (default) → user profile (on click). The GET /api/users list endpoint is new. GET /api/users/:id aggregates identity_mappings and events for a single resolved user.

userStories.title: Final polish: README, error handling, and startup experience
userStories.description: As a new user, I need clear setup instructions and a smooth first-run experience so I can get MiniPanel working without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md contains: project description (one paragraph), prerequisites (Node.js version), setup instructions (npm install, npm run seed, npm run dev), explanation of what each command does
Running 'npm run dev' on a fresh clone (after npm install) starts without errors even if the database does not exist yet (auto-creates it)
If the database is empty, the Events page shows a friendly empty state with a hint to run 'npm run seed'
API endpoints return consistent error response format: { error: string, details?: string }
Server logs incoming requests with method, path, and status code (simple middleware)
No unhandled promise rejections or uncaught exceptions crash the server
Typecheck passes
ALL Playwright tests pass: npx playwright test
userStories.notes: This is the cleanup story. Don't add new features — just make everything that exists work smoothly. The README is the 'single documented command' requirement from BR-103.

userStories.title: Add trend aggregation API endpoint
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need an API that returns event counts grouped by time bucket so the frontend can render trend charts.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/trends returns time-bucketed event counts
Required query parameter: event_name (string, the event type to aggregate)
Optional query parameters: start_date (ISO 8601, default 30 days ago), end_date (ISO 8601, default now), granularity ('day' | 'week', default 'day')
Request is validated with Zod: event_name is required and non-empty, granularity must be 'day' or 'week', start_date must be before end_date if both are provided
Returns 400 with descriptive error if validation fails
Response body: { event_name: string, granularity: string, start_date: string, end_date: string, data: Array<{ date: string, total_count: number, unique_users: number }> }
Each entry in data represents one time bucket (one day or one week starting Monday)
total_count is the number of events in that bucket
unique_users is the count of distinct resolved identities in that bucket — anonymous devices that are mapped to a user_id via identity_mappings are counted under that user_id, not separately
Time buckets with zero events are included in the response with total_count: 0 and unique_users: 0 (no gaps in the series)
Typecheck passes
Trend API basic tests pass: npx playwright test api-trends.spec.ts (daily/weekly bucketing, zero-fill, identity resolution tests)
userStories.notes: The unique_users calculation is the hard part. The SQL needs to resolve identities before counting distinct: for each event, use COALESCE(im.user_id, e.user_id, e.device_id) as the resolved ID, where im is a LEFT JOIN on identity_mappings. Then COUNT(DISTINCT resolved_id). For weekly buckets, use strftime to truncate to ISO week start (Monday). Zero-fill by generating all expected buckets in application code and merging with query results.

userStories.title: Add date range preset helper and validation
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a shared utility for date range presets so the trend page and future features use consistent time ranges.
acceptanceCriteria: A shared utility module exists in the client (e.g., client/src/lib/date-presets.ts)
Exports a DATE_PRESETS array with objects: { label: string, value: string, getDates: () => { start: Date, end: Date } }
Presets include: 'Last 7 days', 'Last 30 days', 'Last 90 days', 'Custom'
'Custom' preset returns null dates indicating the user must pick manually
Each preset calculates dates relative to the current date at call time
Exports a formatDateForAPI(date: Date): string function that returns ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep this pure — no React dependencies. This is a utility module that the Trends page component will import.

userStories.title: Build trends page layout with controls
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need a Trends page with event selector, date range picker, and granularity toggle so I can configure what I want to analyze.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Trends' route added to the router, accessible from the sidebar navigation with a TrendingUp (or BarChart3) lucide-react icon
Page heading: 'Trends'

Control bar at the top contains: event name selector (dropdown populated from GET /api/events/names), date range preset selector (dropdown with 'Last 7 days', 'Last 30 days', 'Last 90 days', 'Custom'), custom date inputs (start and end date pickers, visible only when 'Custom' is selected), granularity toggle ('Day' | 'Week' buttons or segmented control)
Default state on load: no event selected, date range 'Last 30 days', granularity 'Day'
When no event is selected, the chart area shows a prompt: 'Select an event to see its trend'
Selecting an event triggers an API call to GET /api/trends with the current control values
Changing any control (event, date range, granularity) re-fetches data automatically
Loading spinner or skeleton shown while data is being fetched
Uses shadcn/ui Select, Button, and Input components
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Trends page basic UI tests pass: npx playwright test ui-trends.spec.ts (navigation, default state, event selection, chart rendering)
userStories.notes: Don't render the chart yet — that's US-004. This story focuses on the layout, controls, and API wiring. Show raw data or a placeholder where the chart will go.

userStories.title: Render time series chart with Recharts
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need to see event trends as a line chart so I can visually spot patterns and changes over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Install recharts in the client workspace
Trends page renders a responsive line chart using Recharts (ResponsiveContainer + LineChart)
Chart X-axis shows dates from the API response, formatted readably (e.g., 'Jan 5' for daily, 'Jan 5 - Jan 11' for weekly)
Chart has two lines: one for total_count (labeled 'Total Events') and one for unique_users (labeled 'Unique Users')
Lines use distinct colors that are accessible (e.g., blue for total, green for unique)
Chart includes a Tooltip that shows the exact values on hover: date, total events count, unique users count
Chart includes a Legend identifying both lines
Chart Y-axis auto-scales based on data and starts at 0
When the API returns all zeroes, chart still renders with the zero line visible and a subtle message: 'No events found in this range'
Chart is responsive and fills the available width
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Trends page chart tests pass: npx playwright test ui-trends.spec.ts (chart rendering, tooltip, legend tests)
userStories.notes: Use Recharts LineChart — it's already available in the Claude artifact environment and is a well-known React charting library. The two-line chart is the key verification for identity resolution in aggregations: unique_users should be lower than total_count when users trigger the same event multiple times.

userStories.title: Verify event explorer covers BR-200 requirements
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm and fix any gaps between the existing event explorer (from Tier 1) and the BR-200 specification.
acceptanceCriteria: Events page shows events in reverse chronological order (newest first) — verify this is the default sort and fix if not

Each event row displays: timestamp (human-readable format), event name, resolved identity (show user_id if resolved, device_id if anonymous, with a visual indicator for anonymous), all properties (visible on expand or in detail panel)
Filtering by event name works: selecting an event name from the dropdown shows only matching events
Pagination works: 'Previous' and 'Next' controls navigate through large datasets without loading all events at once
Sending an event via the API and then refreshing the events page shows the new event at the top of the list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is a gap-check story. Most of BR-200 was implemented in Tier 1 (US-007 and US-010). This story verifies everything works end-to-end and fixes any issues. The key addition is making sure the resolved identity column is clear — show a label like 'user: abc' vs 'device: xyz (anonymous)' so the developer can verify identity resolution at a glance.

userStories.title: Write automated tests for trend aggregation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the trend API to verify that time bucketing, zero-filling, and identity-resolved unique counts are correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file at server/src/__tests__/trends.test.ts

Test 1 - Daily bucketing: insert events across 3 different days for the same event type, call GET /api/trends with daily granularity, assert each day has the correct total_count and that all days in the range are present (including zero-count days)
Test 2 - Weekly bucketing: insert events across 2 different weeks, call GET /api/trends with weekly granularity, assert events are grouped into the correct week buckets

Test 3 - Unique users with identity resolution: insert 5 events from device-A (anonymous), create identity mapping linking device-A to user-B, insert 3 more events from user-B directly, call GET /api/trends, assert unique_users counts user-B as ONE user not two, while total_count is 8
Test 4 - Zero-fill gaps: insert events only on day 1 and day 5 of a 7-day range, assert response contains 7 entries with days 2-4 and 6-7 having total_count: 0
Test 5 - Date range filtering: insert events outside the requested range, assert they are excluded from results
All tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Test 3 is the critical one — it validates that identity resolution works in aggregations (BR-201 verification point 2). Use in-memory SQLite per test.

userStories.title: Add property metadata API for numeric detection
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need an API that tells me which properties exist for a given event type and whether they are numeric, so the UI can offer appropriate aggregation options.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/events/:eventName/properties returns an array of property descriptors for that event type
Each descriptor has: { name: string, type: 'string' | 'number' | 'boolean', sample_values: string[] }

Type detection logic: scan a sample of recent events (up to 500) for the given event name, for each property key check if all non-null values parse as numbers — if yes, type is 'number'; if all are 'true'/'false', type is 'boolean'; otherwise 'string'
sample_values contains up to 10 distinct values for that property (useful for breakdown selectors)
Properties with no values across all sampled events are excluded
Response is fast (< 500ms) on the sample dataset
Typecheck passes
Property metadata API tests pass: npx playwright test api-properties.spec.ts
userStories.notes: This powers BR-300 (only offer numeric aggregations for numeric properties) and BR-302 (breakdown by property value). Since properties are stored as a JSON string, you need to parse them in SQL or application code. Application-side parsing of a sample is simplest for SQLite.

userStories.title: Extend trend API with numeric aggregation measures
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need the trend API to support sum, average, min, and max of numeric properties so I can measure things like revenue and average order value over time.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/trends now accepts an additional optional query parameter: measure (string, one of 'total_count' | 'unique_users' | 'sum' | 'avg' | 'min' | 'max', default 'total_count')
When measure is 'sum', 'avg', 'min', or 'max', a second required parameter is needed: property (string, the property key to aggregate)
Validated with Zod: if measure is a numeric aggregation, property must be non-empty; if measure is 'total_count' or 'unique_users', property is ignored
Returns 400 if property is specified but contains non-numeric values for the given event type
Response data array entries become: { date: string, value: number } where value is the result of the selected measure for that time bucket
For 'sum': sum of the property values across all events in the bucket
For 'avg': average of the property values across all events in the bucket
For 'min': minimum property value in the bucket
For 'max': maximum property value in the bucket
Buckets with no events return value: 0 for sum, and value: null for avg/min/max (to distinguish 'no data' from 'zero')
Existing total_count and unique_users measures continue to work as before (backward compatible)
Typecheck passes
Trend API numeric measure tests pass: npx playwright test api-trends.spec.ts (sum, avg, min, max tests)
userStories.notes: Since properties are stored as a JSON string column, you'll need to extract the value with json_extract(properties, '$.' || property_name) in SQLite. Cast to REAL for aggregation. Handle NULL and missing property gracefully.

userStories.title: Extend trend API with dimensional breakdown
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need to break down any trend analysis by a property value so I can see separate series for each value (e.g., revenue by plan type).
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/trends now accepts an additional optional parameter: breakdown_by (string, a property key)
When breakdown_by is provided, instead of a single data array, the response returns: { ..., series: Array<{ key: string, data: Array<{ date: string, value: number }> }> }
Each entry in series represents one distinct value of the breakdown property
The series are limited to the top 5 values by total volume (sum of the measure across all time buckets), and all remaining values are grouped into an '__other__' series
Breakdown works with all measures: total_count, unique_users, sum, avg, min, max
When breakdown_by is not provided, the response uses the existing flat data array format (backward compatible)
Zero-fill applies to each series independently (all series have entries for all time buckets)
Typecheck passes
Trend API breakdown tests pass: npx playwright test api-trends.spec.ts (breakdown_by tests)
userStories.notes: This is BR-302. The SQL gets complex: GROUP BY both time bucket and property value, then rank by total volume in application code to determine top 5 vs __other__. Do the ranking and regrouping in TypeScript, not SQL.

userStories.title: Add funnel analysis API endpoint
userStories.description: As a growth manager, I need an API that computes funnel conversion rates for a sequence of events using resolved identities.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/funnels/query accepts a JSON body: { steps: string[] (2-5 event names), start_date: string (ISO 8601), end_date: string (ISO 8601) }
Validated with Zod: steps must have 2-5 entries, each non-empty string; start_date required; end_date required and must be after start_date
The funnel uses resolved identities: for each event, resolve the identity via identity_mappings (same logic as trend unique_users)
A user 'converts' through step N if they performed the step N event AFTER performing the step N-1 event (by timestamp), within the date range
Response body: { steps: Array<{ event_name: string, count: number, conversion_rate: number, drop_off: number }>, overall_conversion_rate: number }
steps[0].count is the number of distinct resolved users who performed the first event in the date range
steps[0].conversion_rate is always 1.0 (100%)
steps[N].conversion_rate is steps[N].count / steps[0].count (relative to funnel entry, not previous step)
steps[N].drop_off is steps[N-1].count - steps[N].count
overall_conversion_rate is steps[last].count / steps[0].count
A user who performs step 1 anonymously and step 2 after identity merge is counted as ONE user progressing (not a dropout)
Typecheck passes
Funnel API tests pass: npx playwright test api-funnels.spec.ts
userStories.notes: This is the most algorithmically complex endpoint. Algorithm: 1) For each step, get all (resolved_user, timestamp) pairs. 2) Starting from step 1 users, for each subsequent step, find users who appear in the next step with a later timestamp. 3) Only users who completed all previous steps can be counted in the next step. Use Sets for efficient intersection. The identity resolution JOIN is the same pattern as trends.

userStories.title: Update trends page with measure and property selectors
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need UI controls to choose what I'm measuring (count, unique users, sum, avg, min, max) and which property to aggregate, so I can analyze numeric properties.
acceptanceCriteria: Trends page control bar adds a 'Measure' dropdown with options: Total Count, Unique Users, Sum, Average, Min, Max
When Sum, Average, Min, or Max is selected, a 'Property' dropdown appears next to it, populated from GET /api/events/:eventName/properties filtered to only numeric properties
When Total Count or Unique Users is selected, the Property dropdown is hidden
Changing the measure or property re-fetches trend data with the new parameters
The chart Y-axis label updates to reflect the measure (e.g., 'Total Events', 'Unique Users', 'Sum of amount')
If no numeric properties exist for the selected event, Sum/Avg/Min/Max options are disabled in the dropdown with a tooltip: 'No numeric properties available'
Default measure remains 'Total Count'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Trends page measure selector tests pass: npx playwright test ui-trends.spec.ts (measure/property selector tests)
userStories.notes: The Property dropdown should only fetch when the event name changes. Cache the property metadata per event name in component state to avoid redundant API calls.

userStories.title: Add breakdown selector to trends page
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need a 'Break down by' dropdown on the trends page so I can split the chart into multiple series by a property value.
acceptanceCriteria: Trends page adds a 'Break down by' dropdown in the control bar, populated from GET /api/events/:eventName/properties (all types, not just numeric)
Dropdown includes a 'None' option (default) that shows the single-series view
When a breakdown property is selected, the API request includes breakdown_by parameter
The chart renders multiple lines (one per series key) with distinct colors from a predefined palette of at least 6 colors
A legend below or beside the chart shows the series key and its color
The '__other__' series is rendered in gray and listed last in the legend
Tooltip shows values for all visible series at the hovered date
Changing the breakdown re-fetches and re-renders the chart
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Trends page breakdown tests pass: npx playwright test ui-trends.spec.ts (breakdown selector, multi-series tests)
userStories.notes: When breakdown is active, the response format changes from flat data[] to series[]. The chart component needs to handle both shapes. Use Recharts' multi-Line approach: map over series and render a <Line> for each.

userStories.title: Add chart type switcher for comparative visualization
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I need to switch between line, bar, and area charts for the same data so I can choose the visualization that best fits my question.
acceptanceCriteria: Trends page adds a chart type toggle in the control bar with three options represented by lucide-react icons: LineChart (line), BarChart3 (bar), AreaChart (area)
Line chart: renders as Recharts LineChart (existing default behavior)
Bar chart: renders as Recharts BarChart with the same data, colors, and tooltip
Area chart: renders as Recharts AreaChart with semi-transparent fills under each line
Switching chart type does NOT re-fetch data — it only changes the rendering
All three chart types work with both single-series and breakdown (multi-series) views
Default chart type is 'line'
Selected chart type is visually highlighted in the toggle
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Chart type switching tests pass: npx playwright test ui-trends.spec.ts (bar, area, line chart toggle tests)
userStories.notes: Abstract the chart data mapping into a shared component or hook so all three chart types use the same data transformation. The main difference is which Recharts component is rendered: LineChart vs BarChart vs AreaChart.

userStories.title: Build funnel analysis page with step builder
userStories.description: As a growth manager, I need a Funnels page where I can define a sequence of events and see conversion rates between steps.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Funnels' route replaces the placeholder, accessible from sidebar with the Filter lucide-react icon
Page heading: 'Funnel Analysis'
Step builder section: user can add 2-5 event steps, each is a dropdown populated from GET /api/events/names
Add Step button (disabled when 5 steps exist), Remove Step button on each step (disabled when 2 steps remain)
Steps are numbered (Step 1, Step 2, etc.) and displayed vertically
Date range selector with the same presets as the Trends page (Last 7 days, Last 30 days, Last 90 days, Custom)
An 'Analyze' button triggers POST /api/funnels/query with the selected steps and date range
Loading state shown while the API processes
Uses shadcn/ui Select, Button, Card components
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Funnel page step builder tests pass: npx playwright test ui-funnels.spec.ts (step builder tests)
userStories.notes: This story is just the input controls and API wiring — the funnel visualization is US-009. Show raw results or a placeholder after the API call.

userStories.title: Render funnel visualization with conversion rates
userStories.description: As a growth manager, I need to see the funnel results as a visual bar chart showing progressive drop-off so I can instantly identify the weakest step.
acceptanceCriteria: After funnel query returns, render a horizontal or vertical bar chart showing each step
Each step bar shows: event name, user count, conversion rate as percentage (relative to step 1), and drop-off count from the previous step
Bars visually decrease in size proportional to the conversion rate (funnel shape)
Between each pair of steps, show the step-to-step conversion rate (e.g., 'Step 1 → Step 2: 65%') and the drop-off count
Overall conversion rate displayed prominently (e.g., 'Overall: 12% of users completed all steps')
Color coding: healthy conversion (>50%) in green, moderate (20-50%) in yellow, poor (<20%) in red
Empty state when no users match the funnel criteria: 'No users completed Step 1 in this date range'
Uses Recharts BarChart or a custom SVG/div-based funnel visualization
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Funnel visualization tests pass: npx playwright test ui-funnels.spec.ts (visualization, conversion rate tests)
userStories.notes: A simple vertical BarChart with decreasing bars works well. Each bar's height (or width) is proportional to the count. The step-to-step annotations are the most important part — they tell the growth manager exactly where users drop off. Prefer a clean custom layout over a generic chart if Recharts BarChart doesn't convey the funnel shape naturally.

userStories.title: Enhance user profile with identity cluster and polished timeline
userStories.description: As a support lead, I need the user profile detail page (reached by clicking a user row) to show the complete identity cluster and a polished event timeline so I can understand a user's full journey across devices.
acceptanceCriteria: User profile at '/users/:id' (reached by clicking a row in the users table) is enhanced with an identity cluster card at the top
Breadcrumb header shows 'Users › {user_id}' with a 'Back to Users' link returning to the table
Identity cluster shows: resolved user_id (primary), all linked device_ids listed as secondary identifiers with a Device lucide-react icon, first_seen and last_seen timestamps in human-readable format
Event timeline below the cluster shows all events attributed to this user in chronological order (oldest first)
Each event in the timeline shows: timestamp, event name, source identity (which device_id or user_id sent it, with a label like 'via device-abc'), all properties as key-value pairs
Events that were originally anonymous (sent with device_id only, later merged) have a subtle visual indicator (e.g., a small 'merged' badge or different background shade)
Timeline supports lazy loading or pagination: initially loads the latest 100 events, 'Load more' button or infinite scroll to fetch earlier events
GET /api/users/:id endpoint is enhanced to also return the identity cluster and event count
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Enhanced user profile tests pass: npx playwright test ui-users-enhanced.spec.ts
userStories.notes: This story enhances the existing US-012 profile detail from Tier 1. The users table from US-012 remains the entry point. The 'via device-abc' annotation is important for the support lead to understand which device generated which event. The merged indicator helps verify identity resolution visually.

userStories.title: Implement global visual coherence pass
userStories.description: As a user, I need MiniPanel to look and feel like a polished product with consistent styling, proper empty states, loading indicators, and error handling across all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a consistent color palette in tailwind.config.ts: primary color (for actions and active states), chart colors (at least 6 distinct colors for multi-series charts), status colors (success green, warning yellow, error red), neutral grays for text hierarchy
Typography hierarchy is consistent: page titles use the same size/weight, section headings use the same size/weight, body text and labels are consistent across all pages
All pages have proper loading states: skeleton loaders or spinners while data is being fetched (Events, Trends, Funnels, User Profile)

All pages have proper empty states with helpful messages: Events page ('No events yet. Run npm run seed to load sample data.'), Trends page ('Select an event to see its trend'), Funnels page ('Define at least 2 steps to analyze a funnel'), User profile ('Enter a user ID or device ID to look up their activity')
All API error responses are caught and displayed as toast notifications or inline error messages (not raw error dumps or blank pages)
Chart labels are readable: axis labels have appropriate font size, dates are formatted consistently, numbers use locale formatting (commas for thousands)
No page shows a blank white screen or broken layout when data is missing or an API call fails
Sidebar active state, hover states, and transitions are smooth and consistent
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is BR-305. It's a polish pass, not a feature. Go through each page systematically: Events, Trends, Funnels, User Profile. Fix inconsistencies. Use shadcn/ui's built-in theming where possible. Add a shared ErrorBoundary component and a shared toast/notification system.

userStories.title: Add saved analyses feature
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I want to save a trend query or funnel configuration with a name so I can reload it later without re-entering all the parameters.
acceptanceCriteria: New database table: saved_analyses with columns: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT), name (TEXT NOT NULL), type ('trend' | 'funnel'), config (TEXT, JSON string storing all query parameters), created_at (TEXT), updated_at (TEXT)
POST /api/saved-analyses accepts { name: string, type: 'trend' | 'funnel', config: object } and stores it, returns the saved record with id
GET /api/saved-analyses returns all saved analyses sorted by updated_at descending
GET /api/saved-analyses/:id returns a single saved analysis
DELETE /api/saved-analyses/:id deletes a saved analysis

On the Trends page: a 'Save' button in the control bar that opens a dialog asking for a name, saves the current query config (event name, measure, property, breakdown, date range, granularity, chart type)
On the Funnels page: a 'Save' button that saves the current funnel config (steps, date range)
A 'Saved Analyses' dropdown or sidebar section on both pages that lists saved analyses of the matching type, clicking one loads its config into the controls and re-runs the query
Uses shadcn/ui Dialog, Input, and Button components
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Saved analyses API tests pass: npx playwright test api-saved.spec.ts
userStories.notes: Tier 4 nice-to-have (BR-400). Config is stored as a JSON blob — no need for a complex schema. Loading a saved analysis means deserializing the config and setting all the control states, then triggering a fetch.

userStories.title: Add autocomplete and search to event and property selectors
userStories.description: As a product analyst working with many event types, I need to search and filter dropdowns so I can find the right event or property quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: All event name dropdowns across the app (Trends, Funnels, Events filter) support type-to-search filtering
All property dropdowns (measure property, breakdown property) support type-to-search filtering
Use shadcn/ui Combobox (Command + Popover pattern) or a searchable Select component
Searching is case-insensitive and matches anywhere in the name (not just prefix)
When the list is long (>10 items), the input auto-focuses for immediate typing
When the list is short (<=10 items), all items are visible without scrolling
No change to API endpoints — filtering is client-side on already-fetched data
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Tier 4 nice-to-have (BR-401). This is a UI-only improvement. Replace all Select components used for event/property selection with the Combobox pattern from shadcn/ui. Build one reusable SearchableSelect component and use it everywhere.

userStories.title: Add multi-event comparison to trends page
userStories.description: As a product analyst, I want to plot multiple event types on the same chart so I can directly compare their volumes or metrics side by side.
acceptanceCriteria: Trends page event selector allows choosing multiple events (multi-select with checkboxes or tags)
When multiple events are selected and no breakdown is active, each event becomes a separate series on the chart with a distinct color and legend entry
The measure applies to each event independently (e.g., 'Total Count' shows count per event, 'Sum of amount' shows sum per event — only events that have the selected property are included)
Maximum 5 events can be compared simultaneously (disable adding more after 5)
Multi-event comparison and breakdown_by are mutually exclusive — selecting a breakdown clears multi-event, and vice versa, with a brief informational message
GET /api/trends is called once per selected event (parallel requests) and results are merged client-side
Legend clearly labels each series with the event name
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Multi-event comparison tests pass: npx playwright test ui-trends.spec.ts (multi-event tests)
userStories.notes: Tier 4 nice-to-have (BR-402). The simplest implementation: fire parallel API calls and merge the results into a multi-series chart. The mutual exclusivity with breakdown keeps complexity manageable — supporting both simultaneously would require a matrix of series.

userStories.title: Write automated tests for numeric aggregations and funnels
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests verifying that numeric aggregations compute correctly and funnel analysis handles identity resolution across steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file at server/src/__tests__/aggregations.test.ts

Test 1 - Sum aggregation: insert 'Purchase Completed' events with known amount values across 3 days, call trends API with measure=sum and property=amount, assert each day's value matches the expected sum
Test 2 - Average aggregation: same events as test 1, call with measure=avg, assert each day's value matches the expected average
Test 3 - Non-numeric property rejected: call trends API with measure=sum on a string property, assert 400 response
Test file at server/src/__tests__/funnels.test.ts
Test 4 - Basic funnel: insert events for 10 users across 3 steps (all 10 do step 1, 7 do step 2, 3 do step 3), call funnel API, assert counts are 10, 7, 3 and conversion rates are 1.0, 0.7, 0.3

Test 5 - Funnel with identity resolution: user does step 1 as anonymous device-X, then identifies as user-Y, then does step 2 as user-Y — assert the funnel counts this as 1 user who completed both steps
Test 6 - Step order enforcement: user does step 2 before step 1 (by timestamp) — assert this user is NOT counted as completing step 2 in the funnel
All tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Test 5 is the critical funnel test — it directly validates BR-303's identity resolution requirement. Test 6 ensures temporal ordering is enforced. Use in-memory SQLite per test.
